 Now I have a good picture of the current codebase. Let me summarize what I see for the audit:

 Key files:
 1. detect-checkpoint-evidence.mjs — The always-on pre-merge gate evidence check. It does check for
    draft_gate + pre_approval_gate comments and unresolved threads. This is supposed to be run before
    merge. The issue #508 says this was bypassed.
 2. conductor-monitor.mjs — Scans async runs, detects orphaned PRs, builds resume plans. It tracks runs by
    scanning session files. It does NOT track active runners per PR or prevent duplicate dispatches.
 3. run-conductor-cycle.mjs — Polls all open PRs, detects state, builds action queue. No runner
    coordination.

 Key observations for the audit:
 - No runner coordination mechanism exists at all — can't track which runner is working on which PR
 - No stale runner detection — no timestamp-based auto-abort
 - No intercom exit signal handling in the loop scripts
 - detect-checkpoint-evidence.mjs exists and works, but was bypassed during the #507 merge
 - The conductor doesn't track active runners vs PR assignments

 For #508 (narrower, S effort):
 - Investigate why detect-checkpoint-evidence.mjs was bypassed
 - Add stale-runner abort guard (timestamp-based)

 For #509 (broader, M effort):
 - Conductor tracks active runner per PR
 - Dispatch fails closed when active runner exists
 - Stale runner detection and auto-abort
 - Pre-merge coordination check
